title: Streams, Super Streams, and Workload Choice · Contract Triage
summary: An incident packet contains a plausible a queue-versus-stream Go client decision record with one stale generation, blocked notification, goroutine leak, acknowledgement, route, or effect defect; locate cause before repair. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a queue-versus-stream Go client decision record and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence de…
objectives: Compare destructive queue consumption with retained stream offsets, replay, retention, protocol clients, and consumer responsibility; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Define age and byte retention, segment behavior, start offset, consumer progress, replay authorization, and late-reader expectations; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Use super streams to partition throughput by key while accounting for partitions, routing, per-partition order, skew, and consumer coordination; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Workload Selection Evidence
steps.instruction:
Order the Workload Selection Evidence RabbitMQ and Go messaging evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Workload Selection Evidence.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Workload Selection Evidence assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For RabbitMQ and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Workload Selection Evidence, keep Workload Selection Evidence connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

content.text: A debug Go messaging team handles case 1198 during race-enabled integration test with duplicate delivery. Build deterministic Go evidence to select queue, stream, or super stream from retention, replay, throughput, and partition evidence; bound payload, queue, confirm-ledger, and handler bytes; then lose a quorum member and recompute majority availability. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Select classic queue, quorum queue, stream, super stream, or external log using loss, latency, throughput, backlog, replay, routing, and operational evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Workload Selection Evidence has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Select classic queue, quorum queue, stream, super stream, or external log using loss, latency, throughput, backlog, replay, routing, and operational evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

For RabbitMQ and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Workload Selection Evidence, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: One RabbitMQ data type is always best if tuned with enough memory and prefetch. In Go, amqp091-go queue consumption automatically exposes every stream protocol capability.

options.text: Declare RabbitMQ and Go outcome, topology, ownership, identity, and byte, time, attempt, and concurrency budgets for the debug Workload Selection Evidence case
Predict RabbitMQ and Go publish, route, custody, delivery, effect, failure, cancellation, and recovery outcomes for the debug Workload Selection Evidence case
Run the smallest deterministic RabbitMQ and Go messaging model and retain inspectable changed-case evidence for the debug Workload Selection Evidence case
Inject one changed RabbitMQ and Go failure, verify bounded cleanup, and state live-broker transfer limits for the debug Workload Selection Evidence case

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Workload Selection Evidence
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: rabbit-go-workload-selection-evidence-77tmom" that uses case 6152 to select queue, stream, or super stream from retention, replay, throughput, and partition evidence. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import amqp091-go, open sockets, contact RabbitMQ, Docker, networks, databases, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5 compile, vet, race, fuzz, leak, amqp091-go 1.12.0, RabbitMQ 4.3, TLS, partition, restart, load, upgrade, and production gates.
